acceptance_criteria: `research:` block parses and validates from `.polyphony-config/profile.yaml`; invalid config produces actionable errors.
Storage abstraction can read from and write to a sibling research repo on the same platform as the source repo (github→github proven end-to-end).
Auth defaults to platform-router credential reuse; no new secret plumbing required for the github→github happy path.
Schema accepts an optional explicit auth reference field without breaking the default path (forward-compat for cross-platform; not exercised here).
Abstraction does not foreclose cross-platform combinations (interface boundary documented).
Unit/integration coverage for schema validation and the storage round-trip.
description:
Introduce the `research:` block in `.polyphony-config/profile.yaml` and the storage abstraction that addresses the sibling research repo. Schema must validate, surface clear errors, and route through polyphony's existing platform-router so a fresh repo can opt in by config alone.

**Auth model (resolved):** the schema defaults to **reusing the platform-router's existing credential resolution** (gh/az) — no per-repo secret references required in the common case. An optional explicit auth reference is accepted for forward-compatibility with cross-platform combinations (e.g. source-on-ADO, research-on-GitHub), but is not required and not exercised in this Epic's minimum bar.
title: `research:` profile config + storage abstraction with platform-router auth reuse

acceptance_criteria: Sub-workflow accepts a `research_needs` payload and returns a synthesized findings document.
`research-assistant` agent is archive-only (no web, GitHub, or MCP tools wired).
End-to-end call works against an empty archive (returns a well-formed "no prior art" findings doc) and a populated archive.
Findings include citations to archived articles with stable references.
Sub-workflow is invocable from a test harness independent of the architect integration.
description:
Add the research sub-workflow skeleton and the cheapest tier — `research-assistant` — which answers `research_needs` strictly from already-archived material in the sibling research repo. No web, no MCP, no extended tools. Proves the call/return loop end-to-end before escalation is wired in.

Model selection defers to polyphony's existing per-agent model resolution — no tier-specific pinning in this Issue.
title: Research sub-workflow + `research-assistant` (archive-only first pass)

acceptance_criteria: `researcher` agent inspects assistant output and emits a structured sufficiency verdict (accept / escalate, with reason).
`deep-researcher` agent has access to extended tools (web, GitHub, MCP) and is invoked only on escalation.
Escalation defaults to 1 per `research_needs` topic; cap is overridable via `research:` profile and the override is validated.
Escalation decisions and tool invocations are logged for cost auditing.
End-to-end test: a topic with no archive prior art escalates exactly once and returns synthesized findings citing fresh sources.
description:
Add the middle and top tiers of the locked three-tier pattern. `researcher` judges sufficiency of the assistant's first pass and either accepts it or escalates to `deep-researcher`, which has extended tools (web, GitHub, MCP) and a stronger model.

**Escalation cap (resolved):** default to **1 escalation per `research_needs` topic**. Overridable via the `research:` profile for users who want a different ceiling. Per-tier model selection defers to polyphony's existing per-agent model resolution.
title: `researcher` sufficiency check + `deep-researcher` escalation (extended tools, default cap = 1)

acceptance_criteria: `archivist` agent classifies each finding as keep / discard / expand with rationale.
Kept articles are written to the sibling research repo with frontmatter and citations (URL + capture date).
Scratch findings land under `research/scratch/<run>/` on the feature branch.
Scratch pruning hook fires at apex close (verification of end-to-end pruning is Stretch per Epic).
Curation pass is idempotent across re-runs of the same `research_needs`.
description: Run an `archivist` curation pass at the end of every research call: keep / discard / expand each scratch finding, and graduate kept articles into the sibling research repo with Obsidian-friendly frontmatter and academic-style citations (URL + capture date minimum). Scratch lives under `research/scratch/<run>/` on the feature branch and is auto-pruned at apex close.
title: `archivist` curation pass + sibling-repo write

acceptance_criteria: Architect output schema accepts a `research_needs` array (topic + rationale).
When `research_needs` is non-empty, the workflow invokes the research sub-workflow and routes findings back into the next architect iteration.
Loop-back path bypasses the plan-review gate.
Findings document is provided to the architect as input context for the next iteration.
End-to-end run on a non-trivial work item demonstrates at least one research loop completing and informing a subsequent plan iteration.
description: Wire the architect agent to emit structured `research_needs` and the workflow to invoke the research sub-workflow, feeding findings back into the next architect iteration. The loop-back must bypass the plan-review gate — research is context, not commitment.
title: Architect integration: `research_needs` emission + loop-back routing bypassing review

acceptance_criteria: Kept articles follow Johnny-Decimal numbering on write.
Frontmatter and wiki-links render correctly in Obsidian.
Citation enforcement: every kept article has at least URL + capture date for each source.
`INDEX.md` is regenerated/updated automatically when articles are added or moved.
A future research call can discover and cite an article written by a prior call (round-trip discoverability proven).
description: Final pass to make accumulated knowledge actually discoverable for the next run: Johnny-Decimal numbering, Obsidian-compatible frontmatter and wiki-links, citation rigor enforcement (URL + capture date minimum), and `INDEX.md` maintenance whenever an article is added or moved.
title: Wiki polish: Johnny-Decimal layout, Obsidian frontmatter/wiki-links, citation rigor, `INDEX.md`
